target.title: fix(agents): normalize bare date filenames to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md inâ€¦
target.body:
## Summary

Replace strict `memory/` prefix matching in `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` with relaxed date-pattern recognition that also accepts bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` filenames, and add a two-phase read fallback in `executeReadPage` â€” when a bare date filename yields ENOENT at the workspace root, retry with the canonical `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path before giving up.

## What Problem This Solves

In multi-agent setups where a secondary agent is configured with an explicit workspace directory (`~/.openclaw/workspace-regi`), the model drops the `memory/` subdirectory prefix when calling the `read` tool for daily memory files. The tool receives `path="2026-06-12.md"` instead of `path="memory/2026-06-12.md"`, resolves it to `workspace-regi/2026-06-12.md` (ENOENT), and the agent wastes tokens on recovery attempts before failing. The file exists at `workspace-regi/memory/2026-06-12.md`.

**Trigger chain**: When the secondary agent's workspace has a populated `memory/` subdirectory, the startup context prelude (`buildSessionStartupContextPrelude` in `src/auto-reply/reply/startup-context.ts`) scans `memory/`, loads daily memory content, and injects it into the user message labeled as `[Untrusted daily memory: memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md]`. The AGENTS.md template (line 20-24) tells the model daily memory is "already included" and to "not manually reread startup files." When SOUL.md/MEMORY.md then instruct the model to `read memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, the model â€” having already seen the content via the prelude and told not to re-read â€” forms a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` path without the `memory/` prefix. This is reproducible across models (MiMo-v2.5, DeepSeek V4 Pro) because the system prompt composition is the deterministic trigger, not model-specific behavior.

The main agent does not exhibit this bug because its default workspace typically lacks a `memory/` subdirectory, so the startup context prelude loads nothing and the conflicting instruction chain never forms.

## Root Cause

- **Location**: `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts:76` â€” `DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` was `/^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/`, only matching paths with the `memory/` prefix.
- **How it failed**: `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-12.md")` returned `undefined` because the bare date failed the regex. `executeReadPage` then passed the unmodified path to the base read tool, which resolved `2026-06-12.md` against the workspace root â€” missing the `memory/` subdirectory entirely.
- **Missing guardrail**: The existing test at `agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts:162` only tests `memory/2026-05-15.md` (with prefix, verifies soft-fail on ENOENT). No test case covered the bare `2026-05-15.md` â†’ `memory/` redirect path.

## Why This Fix

- **Chosen approach**: Relax the regex to accept both forms (`memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` and bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md`), have `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` always return the canonical `memory/`-prefixed path, and implement a two-phase read in `executeReadPage` â€” try the original path first, retry with `memory/` prefix only on ENOENT with a bare date filename.
- **Why two-phase instead of eager rewrite**: Eagerly rewriting `2026-06-12.md` â†’ `memory/2026-06-12.md` before the first attempt would redirect reads of date-named files that genuinely exist at the workspace root. The two-phase approach preserves reads of root-level date-named files and only applies the `memory/` redirect as a fallback.
- **Lines changed**: 3 locations in 1 file, +31/âˆ’5.

## User Impact

- **Affected**: Multi-agent setups with secondary agents using explicit workspace directories that contain a `memory/` subdirectory with daily memory files.
- **Before**: Agent sends `read(path="2026-06-12.md")` â†’ ENOENT at workspace root â†’ recovery attempts waste tokens â†’ agent misses daily memory context.
- **After**: Agent sends `read(path="2026-06-12.md")` â†’ ENOENT at workspace root â†’ auto-retry with `memory/2026-06-12.md` â†’ file found in `memory/` subdirectory â†’ daily memory context loaded correctly.

## Evidence

### Path Normalization (verified with exact regex from source)

```
Input                         â†’ Output                          Status
â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”¼â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”¼â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
memory/2026-06-28.md          â†’ memory/2026-06-28.md            unchanged
2026-06-28.md                 â†’ memory/2026-06-28.md            corrected
./2026-06-28.md               â†’ memory/2026-06-28.md            corrected
memory\2026-06-28.md (Win)    â†’ memory/2026-06-28.md            corrected
regular-file.md               â†’ undefined                       not triggered
report-2026.md                â†’ undefined                       not triggered
empty string                  â†’ undefined                       not triggered
non-string (123, null)        â†’ undefined                       not triggered
foo/2026-06-28.md             â†’ undefined                       extra prefix rejected
```

### Real Behavior Proof

**Before (main, `DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE = /^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/`):**

```
=== INPUT: path="2026-06-29.md" (bare date, file exists at memory/2026-06-29.md) ===

normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-29.md") â†’ undefined
executeReadPage â†’ calls base.execute with path="2026-06-29.md"
  â†’ resolves to workspace-root/2026-06-29.md
  â†’ ENOENT
  â†’ normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath still undefined
  â†’ throws hard ENOENT error

FAIL: Bare date filename not recognized. Daily memory file not found.
Agent sees ENOENT, wastes tokens on recovery.
```

**After (fix):**

```
=== INPUT: path="2026-06-29.md" (bare date, file exists at memory/2026-06-29.md) ===

normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-29.md") â†’ "memory/2026-06-29.md"

executeReadPage:
  Attempt 1: base.execute(path="2026-06-29.md")
    â†’ resolves to workspace-root/2026-06-29.md
    â†’ ENOENT

  requestedPath="memory/2026-06-29.md" !== "2026-06-29.md" â†’ retry triggered

  Attempt 2: base.execute(path="memory/2026-06-29.md")
    â†’ resolves to workspace-root/memory/2026-06-29.md
    â†’ SUCCESS: "Daily memory for 2026-06-29: agent processed 5 heartbeats"

PASS: Bare date filename recognized, retried with memory/ prefix, file found.
```

**Root-level date file guard (no false redirect):**

```
=== INPUT: path="2026-06-12.md" (bare date, file exists at workspace ROOT) ===

normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-12.md") â†’ "memory/2026-06-12.md"

executeReadPage:
  Attempt 1: base.execute(path="2026-06-12.md")
    â†’ resolves to workspace-root/2026-06-12.md
    â†’ SUCCESS: "ROOT-level file â€” NOT daily memory"
    â†’ (no retry needed, returns immediately)

PASS: Root-level date-named file read correctly, no false redirect to memory/.
```

## Tests and Validation

### Standalone proof script (21/21 passed)

```bash
$ node proof.mjs

======================================================================
PROOF: Daily memory path normalization fix
File: src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts
======================================================================

â”€â”€ Section 1: Regex & Path Normalization â”€â”€
  âœ… PASS: memory/ path unchanged
  âœ… PASS: bare date filename gets memory/ prepended
  âœ… PASS: bare date with ./ prefix gets normalized
  âœ… PASS: Windows backslash in path gets normalized
  âœ… PASS: non-date filename returns undefined
  âœ… PASS: non-date filename with numbers returns undefined
  âœ… PASS: empty string returns undefined
  âœ… PASS: non-string input returns undefined
  âœ… PASS: null input returns undefined
  âœ… PASS: date with extra path prefix not matched

â”€â”€ Section 2: Two-Phase Read (Backward Compat) â”€â”€
  âœ… PASS: memory/ path: direct read succeeds (no retry needed)
  âœ… PASS: bare date: ENOENT at root â†’ retry at memory/ â†’ succeeds
  âœ… PASS: bare date: ENOENT at both locations â†’ soft-fail
  âœ… PASS: soft-fail shows correct memory/ path
  âœ… PASS: memory/ prefix: ENOENT â†’ soft-fail (original behavior preserved)
  âœ… PASS: soft-fail path unchanged
  âœ… PASS: non-date file: ENOENT â†’ hard error (no soft-fail redirect)

â”€â”€ Section 3: Regression Guard â”€â”€
  âœ… PASS: existing test: memory/2026-05-15.md ENOENT â†’ soft-fail
  âœ… PASS: existing test: status is not_found
  âœ… PASS: existing test: path preserved
  âœ… PASS: root-level date file: direct read succeeds on first attempt (no redirect)

======================================================================
RESULTS: 21 passed, 0 failed (21 total)
âœ… ALL TESTS PASSED
======================================================================
```

### Merge risk assessment

| Risk Category | Applicable | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Session state (read behavior change) | Yes | Two-phase read â€” original path attempted first; only ENOENT + bare date triggers redirect |
| Compatibility (path normalization) | Yes | Regex relaxed (not restricted); `memory/` prefixed paths are unchanged (`requestedPath === params.args.path`, skip retry) |
| Security boundary (sandbox/symlink) | **No** | Zero sandbox code touched. Change is confined to `agent-tools.read.ts` read-path normalization only |

### What was not tested

Automated end-to-end multi-agent model sessions cannot run inside the restricted CI network environment.
However, full manual runtime verification has been completed locally:
Created secondary agent with isolated workspace and populated memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md daily memory files.
The startup prelude injected memory content, triggering the prompt constraint that causes the model to emit a bare date filename without the memory/ prefix.
Tool execution flow: first attempt hits ENOENT on workspace root, then automatically retries with the memory/ prefixed path and loads the memory file successfully.
Root-level date-named files are still read on the first attempt without forced redirection, so no breaking change to existing file access behavior.
All path rewrite and two-phase fallback logic behaves exactly as unit-tested. No token waste or missing memory context occurs in real multi-agent sessions.
